instruction: Send an email to a public park office asking about picnic reservations. The email must include the adjective "available" in the sense of "able to be used or obtained" and the verb "confirm" in the sense of "establish the truth or correctness of something." Begin the email with the phrase "Dear Park Office," and conclude it with the phrase "Looking forward to your response." The email should contain exactly 150 words, with each sentence containing no more than 20 words. Write the email exclusively in English, incorporating a relevant English idiom. Tailor the email for a general audience, avoiding technical jargon, and provide a brief explanation of why the reservation is needed, including the intended date, number of attendees, and any specific requirements. Avoid using the keyword "forward" in the email.
response:
Dear Park Office,

I hope this message finds you well today. We wish to inquire about picnic table availability for our upcoming family gathering. Our group consists of twelve eager children and four excited adults ready to enjoy the outdoors. We need to confirm the exact number of tables required before our event begins soon. The planned date is next Saturday morning when the weather looks perfect for everyone. We require a large table and a nearby bench for our elderly relatives to sit comfortably. Please let us know if any tables are available during those specific morning hours. We want to ensure our reservation is secure so we do not miss this wonderful opportunity. It is always good to plan ahead when organizing such a fun community event together. We hope you can confirm our request quickly so we can finalize our arrangements properly. Your prompt reply would be greatly appreciated by our entire family group. Thank you very much for your time and helpful assistance with our picnic plans. Looking forward to your response.

Conflicts between Keyword Constraints an.new_constraint: Avoid using the keyword "forward" in the email.
Conflicts between Keyword Constraints an.org_constraint: Conclude the email with the phrase "Looking forward to your response."
